personality_one_liner: A calm, highly readable, and frictionless utility interface that grounds shift workers with clarity and structure.

spacing.rule: Generous padding around content to allow data breathing room. Strict safe area adherence. Thumb-zone compliance for sticky bottom CTAs.

do's: Use on the bottom tab bar when roster grid scrolls behind it.
Use on sticky headers grouping days/weeks in the roster grid.
Apply translucent tinted base (≥70% surface) beneath the blur to ensure foreground readability.
dont's: Do not blur standard list rows or static cards.
Do not use on forms or dense data inputs.

screens.name: Login
screens.purpose: Secure 4-digit PIN entry for shift workers to access their schedules.

states.error: Shake animation on PIN dots + readable error toast.

screens.layout: Hero image banner with top-down gradient scrim at the top half. Bottom half features oversized, numeric keypad using Space Grotesk. 4 circular indicators for PIN status. Safe area padded.

screens.name: Home (Today)
screens.purpose: A glanceable dashboard showing today's status (regular 9hr, short 8.5hr, or day off) with lunch break timing.

states.empty: Illustration of a calm nature workspace indicating a 'Day Off', with a short empowering sentence.
states.loading: Skeleton loaders for today's shift card.
states.error: Retry button with 'Failed to load today's shift' message.

screens.layout: Large hero featured card for 'Today' using brandTertiary tint. Huge typographic emphasis on start/end times. Secondary card below for lunch break details. Sticky CTA if any immediate action is required (e.g., 'Acknowledge Shift').

screens.name: My Roster
screens.purpose: A fortnight (2-week) grid visualizing 9 days of work, 1 day off, and the short day sequence.

states.empty: No upcoming roster generated yet - requires admin action.

screens.layout: Scrollable table/grid view. Columns for days, rows for week 1 and week 2. Use distinct color-coding for shifts: solid brandPrimary for regular 9hr, warning/amber tint for 8.5hr short day, and surfaceTertiary/empty state for the day-off. Sticky header with glassmorphism for days of the week.

screens.name: Settings / Edit Roster
screens.purpose: Allow users to edit their base roster settings (working days, next day-off date).
screens.layout: Single-column form layout. Labels above inputs. Use tinted icon containers for setting rows. Sticky primary CTA 'Save Changes' at the bottom over scrolling content.

screens.name: Admin Dashboard
screens.purpose: Overview of all users and their respective rosters for managers.

states.empty: Illustration indicating 'No employees added yet' with 'Add Employee' primary CTA.
states.error: Error banner at the top of the list.

screens.layout: List view of employees. Each row has an initials avatar (or profile image) in a brandTertiary circle, name, and current week's hour progress. Tapping a row opens a bottom sheet with their full 2-week grid.

empty_day_off.alt: Small green succulent in a white pot representing a calm day off

instructions_to_main_agent: Design strictly adheres to '1 iOS-Native Clean' personality. Do not use Brutalist heavy borders or generic blue/indigo SaaS colors.
The primary brand color is a calming Moss Green (#425948) to convey stability without being a generic corporate blue. Ensure strict WCAG contrast for all text.

For the Fortnight Roster Grid, use a clean scrollable calendar grid format. Clearly differentiate the 9hr, 8.5hr (short day), and Day-Off markers using tonal color codes (e.g., solid brand for 9hr, amber/warning tint for short day, grayed out for day off).
Typography: Use 'Space Grotesk' exclusively for numeric data (clock times, PIN pad, shift durations) and 'Plus Jakarta Sans' for standard text to ensure maximum readability.
All images used as backgrounds MUST implement a top-to-bottom vertical gradient scrim (transparent to ≥70% surfaceInverse) to guarantee foreground text readability.
Maintain generous padding (lg/xl spacing) around components. The roster app needs to feel organized and uncluttered.
Use glassmorphism (expo-blur) for the bottom tab bar and sticky headers above the scrolling calendar grid.
Ensure every screen has explicit loading, empty, and error states built-in, occupying the full flex:1 space.
